news.summary: SFC has taken over the management of 15 facilities across 11 states as part of an agreement with Rink Management Services. The agreement, announced on May 22, 2025, allows The Sports Facilities Companies to enhance its portfolio in managing sports, recreation, and event venues. This expansion includes an increase in ice rink facilities under their management.
news.headline: The Sports Facilities Companies adds 13 new ice properties and 2 community centers in 11 states, now managing 15 facilities.

news.summary: In BROSSARD, Quebec, on May 20, 2025, Diagnos Inc. (TSX Venture: ADK, OTCQB: DGNOF, FWB: 4D4A), a leader in early health issue detection through AI technology, plans to prolong the maturity date for $300,000 unsecured convertible debentures from a private unit placement disclosed on May 18, 2023. Originally due on May 18, 2025, the maturity date will now be May 18, 2026 as stipulated in the Amendment.
news.headline: DIAGNOS extends $300,000 convertible debentures maturity date to May 18, 2026, part of a private placement.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has reached an all-time high of 14.7%, with over 20 million people currently unemployed. This spike in joblessness is largely attributed to the economic consequences of the ongoing global pandemic. Additionally, the report shows a significant decrease in consumer spending, dropping by 16.4% in the last month alone. The government is implementing various measures to counteract these challenges, including stimulus packages and financial assistance programs for affected individuals and businesses. Experts predict a slow recovery for the economy, emphasizing the need for continued support and intervention.
news.headline: Analysis: Potential winners and losers in Trump's Fannie and Freddie proposal.

news.summary: In Guangzhou, China, on May 20, 2025, Top KingWin Ltd revealed that it has met the Nasdaq requirements. The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC informed the Company on May 19, 2025, that it is now in compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2). This rule mandates that the Company's class A ordinary shares, valued at US$0.0025 per share, must uphold a minimum bid price of $1.00 per share.
news.headline: Top KingWin Ltd regains compliance with Nasdaq minimum closing bid price rule by maintaining $1.00 per share.

news.summary: On May 20, 2025, Vaxart, Inc. revealed that its Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors sanctioned equity awards for the newly appointed Chief Financial Officer, Jeroen Grasman, starting from May 19, 2025. These awards were granted in accordance with Mr. Grasman's offer letter and to entice him to assume the role of Chief Financial Officer. The company is listed as Nasdaq: VXRT.
news.headline: Vaxart, Inc. grants equity awards to new CFO, Jeroen Grasman, under Nasdaq rules. Effective May 19, 2025.

news.summary: Stay updated on the most recent developments from Alphabet, the parent company of Google, with in-depth analysis and quotes about the search giant's stock performance. Gain insights into Alphabet's stock quotes, company news, and chart analysis through Investor's Business Daily. Explore the latest information regarding Alphabet (GOOGL) to make informed investment decisions. Discover key details about the company's performance and stock trends to stay ahead in the market.
news.headline: Stay updated with Google-parent Alphabet (GOOGL) stock news & chart analysis from Investor's Business Daily.

news.summary: President Trump has been promoting massive investment commitments as evidence of a booming economy, but a new analysis by Goldman Sachs indicates the actual economic impact might be less significant. Foreign entities have pledged a total of $6 trillion in U.S. investments, with $2 trillion from companies and $4.2 trillion from governments. Recent commitments include $2 trillion from Gulf nations and $1 trillion from Japan. Major pledges from companies include Nvidia Corp. ($787 billion), IBM ($409 billion), Apple Inc. ($302 billion), and Eli Lilly & Co. ($35 billion). However, Goldman Sachs predicts a smaller impact on U.S. investment than expected.
news.headline: Trump's $6 Trillion investment deals face skepticism, as Goldman Sachs analysis shows modest actual economic impact.

news.summary: Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. announced the successful upsizing of its offering, pricing approximately $2.3 billion of senior notes, up from the initially planned $2 billion. The company plans to use the net proceeds for a Tender Offer to purchase various senior notes due between 2026 and 2031, as well as to cover associated fees and expenses, and potentially repay outstanding debt. The net proceeds may also be temporarily invested until utilized for their intended purposes.
news.headline: Teva prices $2.3B senior notes, upsized from $2B; aims to repay existing debt and fund tender offers.

news.summary: Fairfax Financial Holdings Limited, based in Toronto, has successfully concluded a private offering of senior notes. The offering consisted of US$500 million in 5.750% senior notes due in 2035 and US$400 million in 6.500% senior notes due in 2055. These notes were issued under the tickers FFH and FFH.U on the Toronto Stock Exchange.
news.headline: Fairfax wraps up US$900M Senior Notes Offering with 5.75% notes due 2035 and 6.5% notes due 2055.

news.summary: This week, discussions on trade and economic matters will take place involving Scott Bessent, the Treasury secretary, and Jamieson Greer, the United States trade representative, meeting with officials.
news.headline: U.S. and China to conduct initial trade talks after tariffs by Treasury secretary and trade representative.

news.summary: The President has ambitious plans to revive America's manufacturing sector and bring back its former glory. However, historical economic patterns suggest that altering these cycles is extremely challenging.
news.headline: Can Trump revitalize US manufacturing? Challenges in overturning economic trends are significant.

news.summary: In the latest data released, it was reported that the unemployment rate in the country has risen to 7.9%, with over 12.6 million individuals currently out of work. This marks a significant increase from last month's rate of 6.7% and has sparked concerns about the state of the economy. Additionally, the report highlighted a decrease in job creation, with only 245,000 new jobs being added last month compared to the expected 500,000. Experts are projecting a slow recovery for the labor market in the coming months as the country continues to navigate the ongoing challenges posed by the pandemic.
news.headline: Trump's proposed tariffs are so significant that a 10% decrease feels like a welcomed relief.

news.summary: A potential agreement would be beneficial for both administrations, as they have been seeking a deal since President Trump's initial term in office.
news.headline: US to announce trade deal with UK. Both gov'ts have eyed this agreement since Trump's first term, signaling positivity.

news.summary: The countries with nuclear capabilities are in conflict over Pakistan's access to essential international aid amid India's efforts to utilize its influence and connections to disrupt its historical rival.
news.headline: India is attempting to weaken Pakistan's influence through cuts in foreign aid and strategic diplomatic maneuvers.

news.summary: In New York, a recent survey by Cyware highlighted the critical nature of threat intelligence, with a staggering 92% of respondents emphasizing its importance. Despite this widespread recognition, the survey also exposed a significant challenge faced by many organizations - the struggle to effectively operationalize threat intelligence. This highlights a gap in implementation that could potentially hinder cybersecurity efforts.
news.headline: 92% believe threat intelligence is critical, but organizations struggle to operationalize it.

news.summary: The agreement revealed by President Trump on Thursday requires further negotiations, but the administration stated that the deal with a top American ally marks the beginning of numerous agreements to follow.
news.headline: New trade deal between U.S. and U.K. to maintain close ties with some tariffs in place. Trump's administration plans more agreements.

news.summary: A recent report shows that the global economy is expected to grow by 5.6% in 2021, a significant increase from the 3.5% growth registered in 2020. This boost is largely attributed to the rollout of COVID-19 vaccines and the easing of pandemic-related restrictions in many countries. Advanced economies are projected to expand by 5.4%, while emerging markets and developing economies are forecasted to grow by 6%.However, despite the overall positive outlook, disparities remain among regions and countries. The recovery is expected to be uneven, with advanced economies recovering faster than developing ones. The report emphasized the importance of equitable vaccine distribution to ensure a sustainable global economic recovery.In terms of sectors, the report highlights that the services sector, particularly industries such as tourism and hospitality, continues to be heavily impacted by the pandemic. This sector is projected to grow by 4.9%, significantly lower than the 6.4% growth expected in the industrial sector.Overall, while the global economy is poised for a rebound in 2021, challenges persist, and coordinated efforts will be essential to support a more inclusive and sustainable recovery.
news.headline: ARBA Retail Systems uses AI to revolutionize retail industry. #AI #Retail #Innovation

news.summary: On Tuesday afternoon, the Dow Jones Industrial Average and other key indexes struggled to recover from losses. A prominent financial services stock experienced a significant decline on today's stock market. During the day's trading session, the Dow Jones fell along with the S&P 500, halting its winning streak. Notably, a coffee kiosks stock also faced a substantial decrease.
news.headline: Stock market today sees Dow Jones falling, S&P 500 ends win streak; Coffee kiosks stock plunges. Dow clings to losses.

news.summary: During the first quarter of 2025, activist investor Dan Loeb's Third Point LLC significantly increased its investment in Apollo Global Management, Inc. (NYSE: APO) and Soho House & Co Inc. (NYSE: SHCO). As per recent 13F filings as of March 31, 2025, Loeb now holds 1.5 million shares of Apollo, up from 400,000 in the fourth quarter of 2024, marking a 275% increase. This turnaround follows a decrease in Apollo shares from 950,000 in Q3 2024 to 400,000 in Q4 2024. Loeb also augmented his stake in Soho House to 5.2 million shares in Q1 2025 from 2.35 million in Q4 2024. For further details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Loeb's Third Point significantly raises stake in Apollo (+275%) and Soho House (+121%) in Q1 2025, per 13F filings.

news.summary: On May 22, 2025, a press release was issued for shareholders of Cohen & Steers Total Return Realty Fund, Inc. (NYSE: RFI), offering details about the upcoming distribution to be made on May 30, 2025. The release includes information about the sources of the distribution and cumulative distributions paid so far in the fiscal year.
news.headline: Cohen & Steers Total Return Realty Fund (RFI) provides info on sources of distribution for May 30, 2025, to shareholders.

news.summary: An American company under U.S. government scrutiny due to its connections to China is seeking to acquire hundreds of thousands of advanced artificial intelligence chips. The deal, potentially to be completed this week, marks a significant move for the firm.
news.headline: US mulls selling large number of high-tech chips to Emirati A.I. company G42 under scrutiny for Chinese connections. Deal expected this week.

In addition to this, the platform is also featuring predictions on the performance of Coinbase and box office figures. This has attracted significant attention from users interested in cryptocurrency and financial markets, with many eager to participate in these predictions. The potential outcome of these forecasts has stirred excitement among users, with numerous individuals closely monitoring the numbers to see if the predictions come to fruition.
news.headline: Bitcoin's future price debated - Will it surpass $115K this weekend? Coinbase hack bounty prize draws attention.

news.summary: Johnson & Johnson has received FDA approval for its anti-FcRn antibody, nipocalimab, to treat generalized myasthenia gravis. Marketed as IMAAVY, the drug's approval strengthens J&J's position against competitors in the myasthenia gravis market. This move highlights the company's commitment to innovation in treatment options.
news.headline: FDA approves Johnson & Johnson's IMAAVY for myasthenia gravis, poses challenge to top players.

news.summary: On May 22, 2025, Cohen & Steers Quality Income Realty Fund, Inc. (NYSE: RQI) issued a press release for its shareholders, detailing the distribution payment set for May 30, 2025. news.summary: Critics from both sides of the political aisle are denouncing the controversial practice of offering investors access to the president in exchange for backing his digital coin. Democrats and a portion of Republicans are labeling this move as unethical and detrimental to the integrity of the political system.
news.headline: Winners of Trump’s Memecoin contest gather for dinner, sparking controversy due to unethical sale of access to the president.

news.summary: An American tourist in the UK was drugged by a fake taxi driver who stole $123,000 in Bitcoin from his phone. After drinking at a London bar, Jacob Irwin-Cline mistakenly got into a private cab, thinking it was his Uber. The driver gave him a sedative-laced cigarette, making him pass out for 30 minutes. Upon waking up, the driver ordered Cline out, then hit him with the car and fled with his phone containing crypto access. This incident reflects a series of crimes against crypto industry figures, including kidnappings and extortion attempts. In May, a crypto exchange owner's father was held captive in France, while the Paymium CEO's family faced a kidnapping attempt in Paris. The surge in violent attacks on crypto professionals has led to heightened security measures.
news.headline: Fake Uber driver drugs US tourist in UK, robs $123K BTC. Incidents of crypto exec kidnappings rising. Paris attacks heighten security concerns.

TRUMP TO INVOKE WARTIME ACT OVER US URANIUM DEPENDENCE

news.summary: Senate Democrats, including Chuck Schumer, Elizabeth Warren, and Jeff Merkley, are proposing an amendment to the GENIUS Act to prevent a U.S. president, including Donald Trump, from profiting from stablecoins like the USD1. Trump and his three sons are involved in the crypto platform World Liberty Financial, which launched USD1 in March. Critics argue that allowing stablecoins to be recognized as financial instruments in the U.S. could benefit Trump personally. A firm used USD1 to settle a $2 billion investment in Binance, potentially profiting the president's family. Democrats are also concerned about Trump hosting a dinner for those who purchased his memecoin, with attendees remaining mostly anonymous. Schumer and Warren plan responses to this conflict of interest, calling for transparency and raising concerns about potential corruption and insider trading. The connection between Trump's crypto ventures and his presidential influence is under scrutiny, with Democrats advocating for investigations and reforms.
news.headline: Senators aim to amend GENIUS Act to prevent Trump family profiting from stablecoin; Dems plan response to Trump's crypto connections - bill advanced by Senate with 18 Dem votes on May 20, USD1 stablecoin launched by WLFI in March, Dems call for investigation into Trump's ties to platform. Warren and Merkley planning actions in response to Trump hosting dinner for high memecoin purchasers at golf club. Protest expected outside the venue, Dems urge Trump to release guest list. Critics highlight potential conflicts of interest.

news.summary: Symbolized as TSX/NYSE AMERICAN: SVM, Vancouver-based Silvercorp Metals Inc. disclosed its financial and operational performance for Q4 Fiscal 2025 and Fiscal 2025. The company, known as Silvercorp, traded under the tickers TSX: SVM and NYSE American: SVM. This data showcases its quarterly and annual results for the stated periods.
news.headline: Silvercorp reports adjusted net income of $75.1M, $0.37 per share, and cash flow of $138.6M for FY 2025.

news.summary: Intouch Insight Ltd reported their financial results for the quarter ending March 31, 2025. They saw significant improvements in net earnings before taxes, reaching $289,254, a 414% increase from $69,804 in Q1 2024. EBITDA also rose to $557,748 in Q1 2025 compared to $343,843 the prior year. Recurring services revenue slightly increased by 2% to $5,359,439. Total revenue decreased by 19% to $6,319,663 due to the elimination of low-margin revenue streams. Gross margin dollars increased by 4% to $3,190,515 with a growth in margin percentage to 50.5% from 39.1%. The company's CEO, Cameron Watt, attributed these positive results to exiting low-margin revenue streams in Q1 2024. Looking ahead, the company aims to drive growth through expanding recurring services, SaaS offerings, and utilizing AI for differentiation, automation, and cost improvements. They are also considering re-entering the merchandising business.
news.headline: Intouch Insight's Q1 2025 financial results show net earnings before taxes of $289,254, an EBITDA of $557,748, and 2% increase in recurring services revenue to $5,359,439. Gross margin percentage increased by 11% to 50.5%. CEO highlights exiting low-margin revenue streams and pursuing growth opportunities.
TRUMP TO SIGN ORDERS TO BOOST NUCLEAR POWER AS SOON AS FRIDAY, SOURCES SAY

news.summary: On May 22, 2025, Soleno Therapeutics, Inc. revealed that its Marketing Authorization Application (MAA) for Diazoxide Choline Prolonged-Release Tablets, targeted at treating individuals aged four and above with Prader-Willi syndrome (PWS) and hyperphagia, has been validated by the European Medicines Agency (EMA). Soleno, a biopharmaceutical company, is focused on developing innovative therapies for rare diseases. The company's shares are listed on the NASDAQ under the symbol SLNO. This move marks a significant step forward in potentially providing relief for individuals affected by PWS, which is a rare genetic disorder.
news.headline: Soleno submits MAA for Diazoxide Choline Tablets for hyperphagia in PWS patients aged 4+, validated by EMA.

news.summary: A recent study showcasing the effectiveness of the one-time Amtagvi treatment revealed enduring responses among patients, along with a notable five-year overall survival rate of 20%. This indicates promising outcomes for individuals undergoing this treatment, as it exhibits long-lasting benefits and a relatively positive survival rate over the course of five years.
news.headline: Iovance presents 5-year results of Amtagvi in advanced melanoma at ASCO, showing 20% overall survival rate.

news.summary: On May 22, 2025, a recent announcement was made for shareholders of Cohen & Steers Infrastructure Fund, Inc. (NYSE: UTF). The press release contains details about the upcoming distribution scheduled for May 30, 2025, shedding light on where the funds are sourced from. Additionally, it provides an overview of the total distributions made so far in the fiscal year. Shareholders can expect more specific information in the official communication.
news.headline: Cohen & Steers Infrastructure Fund (UTF) releases sources of distribution for shareholders ahead of May 30, 2025 payout.

news.summary: Sanofi has officially opened its new main offices in the US on May 22, 2025. The new state-of-the-art facility in Morristown, New Jersey aims to promote collaboration and innovation. The office is set up to facilitate the development and delivery of medicines and vaccines to patients.
news.headline: Sanofi unveils $130M NJ offices, boosts US presence with innovative design for drug development.

news.summary: A series of studies led by experts will showcase advancements in various types of cancer and explore practical insights influencing the direction of personalized cancer care. This event will take place in New Brunswick, N.J., starting on May 22, 2025. The studies will involve clinicians and researchers from RWJBarnabas Health and Rutgers Cancer Institute.
news.headline: RWJBarnabas Health & Rutgers Cancer Institute to share research at 2025 ASCO Annual Meeting, impacting personalized oncology.

news.summary: About a year after the FDA initially denied its approval submission, Abeona revealed that the agency has now given the green light for ZEVASKYN. This autologous cell-based gene therapy is authorized for use in adult and pediatric patients with RDEB. The approval signifies a significant milestone for Abeona as it marks their official foray into the commercial gene therapy market.
news.headline: Abeona gains FDA approval for ZEVASKYN gene therapy for RDEB, marking its entry into the market after a previous rejection.

news.summary: The outcomes of the KOMET-001 registration-directed trial regarding ziftomenib in relapsed/refractory NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia (AML) patients have been chosen for presentation at an oral session on Monday, June 2nd. This significant development indicates progress in the study of this drug and its potential benefits for patients facing this type of AML. Stay tuned for more details on the latest findings and advancements in the field.
news.headline: Kura Oncology and Kyowa Kirin present pivotal data on Ziftomenib at 2025 ASCO Annual Meeting for AML patients.
Merus’ Petosemtamab and Pembrolizumab show strong results in treating PD-L1+ r/m HNSCC in first-line, with durable efficacy.

news.summary: Thursday afternoon saw minimal movement in the Dow Jones and other major indexes, remaining mostly stagnant. Notably, a cosmetic company's stock bounced back to approach a new peak. Live updates covered the day's stock market activity, with the Dow Jones and S&P 500 experiencing slight declines, while an IBD 50 stock surged to reach a fresh high.
news.headline: Stock indexes flat, with Dow Jones slightly down. IBD 50 stock hits new high. Beauty stock near peak.

news.summary: Founder and host of Turning Point Broadcast Ministry, Dr. David Jeremiah, has released a statement regarding the recent attack on Israeli embassy staff. The incident took place in San Diego on May 22, 2025. Dr. Jeremiah expressed his sorrow over the violence, emphasizing that any form of violence is deeply saddening. The attack has left many bewildered as to why such events persist.
news.headline: Founder of Turning Point Broadcast Ministry condemns attack on Israeli Embassy, calls for peace.
